frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Bhaiya, aajkal shaadi karna bhi mushkil ho gaya hai. Ab toh sunte hain agar by chance na bani, toh ladkiyan case kar deti hain aur aadhe-aadhe hisse ki baat karti hain. Bechara ladka, sab uski mehnat ka. Pehle kahan aisa hota tha?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment generalizes that 'girls' (ladkiyan) exploit legal avenues like divorce to claim 'half shares' (aadhe-aadhe hisse) of a man's earnings, framing it as a misfortune for the man ('Bechara ladka'). It encodes the core belief of women being golddiggers and uses victimhood inversion for men, fitting the 'alimony as reverse dowry' manifestation.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 52. A farmer in rural UP. OBC Hindu. Your 17 year old daughter Rekha has topped her class 12 board exams and her teacher has come to your house personally to tell you she should apply to colleges in Lucknow. You have listened to the teacher politely. The teacher has left. You are now sitting with your brother in the courtyard. You are telling your brother what the teacher said and what you have decided and why you think sending Rekha to a college 90 kilometres away is not something you are going to do and what you think education beyond a point does to girls from families like yours.
